passage_title: Thomas Young The Last True Know-It-All

sentences.sentence: Thomas Young (1773-1829) contributed 63 articles to the Encyclopedia Britannica, including 46 biographical entries (mostly on scientists and classicists) and substantial essays on "Bridge,""Chromatics," "Egypt," "Languages" and "Tides".
sentences.translation: 托马斯·杨（1773—1829）为《大英百科全书》撰写了63篇文章，包括46篇人物传记（大多关于科学家和古典学者），以及关于“桥梁”“色彩学”“埃及”“语言”和“潮汐”的重要论文。

structure.text: Thomas Young (1773-1829)
contributed 63 articles to the Encyclopedia Britannica
including 46 biographical entries (mostly on scientists and classicists) and substantial essays on "Bridge,""Chromatics," "Egypt," "Languages" and "Tides"

difficult_points: including ... 补充列举63篇文章的构成。
原文 "Bridge," 与 "Chromatics" 两个引号之间没有空格，须保留。

sentences.sentence: Was someone who could write authorita-tively about so many subjects a polymath, a genius or a dilettante?
sentences.translation: 一个能对如此多主题作出权威论述的人，究竟是博学家、天才，还是涉猎广而不精的业余者？

structure.text: Was
someone
who could write authorita-tively about so many subjects
a polymath, a genius or a dilettante

difficult_points: 系动词 Was 提到主语前构成疑问句；who 从句修饰 someone。
原文 authorita-tively 被错误断词，必须保留。

sentences.sentence: In an ambitious new biog-raphy, Andrew Robinson argues that Young is a good contender for the epitaph "the last man who knew everything."
sentences.translation: 安德鲁·罗宾逊在一部雄心勃勃的新传记中主张，杨很有资格获得“最后一个无所不知的人”这一称号。

structure.text: In an ambitious new biog-raphy
Andrew Robinson argues
that Young is a good contender for the epitaph "the last man who knew everything."
who knew everything

difficult_points: that 从句作 argues 的宾语，who 从句修饰 man。
原文 biog-raphy 被错误断词，epitaph 在此指概括人物一生的称号。

expressions.text: the last man who knew everything
expressions.meaning_zh: 最后一个无所不知的人

sentences.sentence: Young has competition, however: The phrase, which Robinson takes for his title, also serves as the subtitle of two other recent biographies: Leonard Warren's 1998 life of paleontologist Joseph Leidy (1823-1891) and Paula Findlen's 2004 book on Athanasius Kircher (1602-1680), another polymath.
sentences.translation: 不过，杨也有竞争者：罗宾逊用作书名的这个短语，也被另外两部近年传记用作副标题，分别是伦纳德·沃伦1998年为古生物学家约瑟夫·莱迪所作的传记，以及宝拉·芬德伦2004年关于另一位博学家阿塔纳修斯·基歇尔的著作。

structure.text: Young has competition
The phrase
which Robinson takes for his title
also serves as the subtitle of two other recent biographies
Leonard Warren's 1998 life of paleontologist Joseph Leidy (1823-1891) and Paula Findlen's 2004 book on Athanasius Kircher (1602-1680), another polymath

difficult_points: 第一个冒号引出竞争者的解释，第二个冒号列出两部传记。
which 修饰 phrase；another polymath 与 Athanasius Kircher 同位。

sentences.sentence: Young, of course, did more than write encyclopedia entries.
sentences.translation: 当然，杨所做的远不止撰写百科全书条目。

structure.text: Young
of course
did more than write encyclopedia entries

difficult_points: did more than 强调其成就超出撰写词条。
of course 是插入语。

sentences.sentence: He presented his first paper to the Royal Society of London at the age of 20 and was elected a Fellow a week after his 21st birthday.
sentences.translation: 他20岁时向伦敦皇家学会提交首篇论文，并在21岁生日一周后当选会士。

structure.text: He
presented his first paper to the Royal Society of London
and was elected a Fellow

difficult_points: presented 与 was elected 是主动、被动并列谓语。
at the age of 20 与 a week after ... 分别限定两个事件时间。

sentences.sentence: In the paper, Young explained the process of accommodation in the human eye —on how the eye focuses properly on objects at varying distances.
sentences.translation: 杨在论文中解释了人眼的调节过程，即眼睛如何正确聚焦于不同距离的物体。

structure.text: Young explained the process of accommodation in the human eye
on how the eye focuses properly on objects at varying distances

difficult_points: 破折号后解释 accommodation 的具体含义。
原文 —on 的介词搭配略生硬，须保留。

sentences.sentence: Young hypothesised that this was achieved by changes in the shape of the lens.
sentences.translation: 杨假设，这一调节是通过晶状体形状的变化实现的。

structure.text: Young hypothesised
that this was achieved by changes in the shape of the lens

difficult_points: that 从句作 hypothesised 的宾语。
this 指眼睛调焦，was achieved by 是被动结构。

sentences.sentence: Young also theorised that light traveled in waves and ho believed that, to account for the ability to see in color, there must be three receptors in the eye corresponding to the three "principal colors" to which the retina could respond: red, green, violet.
sentences.translation: 杨还提出光以波的形式传播，并认为，为解释彩色视觉能力，眼中必有三种感受器，分别对应视网膜能响应的三种“基本颜色”：红、绿、紫。

structure.text: Young also theorised
that light traveled in waves
and ho believed
that, to account for the ability to see in color, there must be three receptors in the eye
corresponding to the three "principal colors"
to which the retina could respond

difficult_points: 两个 that 从句分别作 theorised 和 believed 的宾语。
corresponding ... 修饰 receptors，to which 修饰 colors 并提前介词。
原文 ho 很可能是 he，color/colors 使用美式拼写，均保留。

sentences.sentence: All these hypotheses Were subsequently proved to be correct.
sentences.translation: 所有这些假说后来都被证明是正确的。

structure.text: All these hypotheses
Were subsequently proved to be correct

difficult_points: be proved to be 是被动主语补足语结构。
原文 Were 异常大写，必须保留。

expressions.text: be subsequently proved to be correct

sentences.sentence: Later in his life, when he was in his forties, Young was instrumental in cracking the code that unlocked the unknown script on the Rosetta Stone, a tablet that was "found" in Egypt by the Napoleonic army in 1799.
sentences.translation: 晚年四十多岁时，杨在破解罗塞塔石碑未知文字的密码方面发挥了关键作用；这块石碑于1799年被拿破仑军队在埃及“发现”。

structure.text: when he was in his forties
Young was instrumental in cracking the code
that unlocked the unknown script on the Rosetta Stone
a tablet
that was "found" in Egypt by the Napoleonic army in 1799

difficult_points: be instrumental in 后接动名词，表示发挥关键作用。
两个 that 分别修饰 code 和 tablet；a tablet 与 Rosetta Stone 同位。

sentences.sentence: The stone contains text in three alphabets: Greek, something unrecognisable and Egyptian hieroglyphs.
sentences.translation: 石碑包含三种文字：希腊文、一种无法识别的文字以及埃及象形文字。

structure.text: The stone
contains text in three alphabets
Greek, something unrecognisable and Egyptian hieroglyphs

difficult_points: 冒号后具体列出 three alphabets。
something unrecognisable 指当时尚未识别的世俗体文字。

sentences.sentence: The unrecognisable script is now known as demotic and, as Young deduced, is related directly to hieroglyphic.
sentences.translation: 这种原本无法识别的文字如今被称为世俗体；正如杨所推断，它与象形文字有直接关系。

structure.text: The unrecognisable script
is now known as demotic
as Young deduced
is related directly to hieroglyphic

difficult_points: 两个 is 谓语共用主语 script。
as Young deduced 是插入语，意为正如杨推断。

sentences.sentence: His initial work on this appeared in his Britannica entry on Egypt.
sentences.translation: 他对此的初步研究发表在《大英百科全书》的“埃及”词条中。

structure.text: His initial work on this
appeared in his Britannica entry on Egypt

difficult_points: this 指世俗体文字与象形文字的关系。
entry on Egypt 指百科全书中关于埃及的词条。

sentences.sentence: In another entry, he coined the term Indo-European to describe the family of languages spoken throughout most of Europe and northern India.
sentences.translation: 在另一词条中，他创造了“印欧语系”一词，用于描述欧洲大部分地区和印度北部所使用的语言家族。

structure.text: he coined the term Indo-European
to describe the family of languages
spoken throughout most of Europe and northern India

difficult_points: coin the term 表示创造术语。
spoken ... 修饰 languages，句首不定式表示术语用途。

sentences.sentence: These are the landmark achievements of a man who was a child prodigy and who, unlike many remarkable children, did not disappear into oblivion as an adult.
sentences.translation: 这些是一位神童的里程碑式成就；与许多杰出儿童不同，他成年后并未销声匿迹。

structure.text: These are the landmark achievements of a man
who was a child prodigy
and who, unlike many remarkable children, did not disappear into oblivion as an adult

difficult_points: 两个 who 从句并列修饰 a man。
disappear into oblivion 表示不再为人所知。

sentences.sentence: Bom in 1773 in Somerset in England, Young lived from an early age with his maternal grandfather, eventually leaving to attend boarding school.
sentences.translation: 杨于1773年出生在英格兰萨默塞特郡，自幼与外祖父生活，后来离家进入寄宿学校。

structure.text: Bom in 1773 in Somerset in England
Young lived from an early age with his maternal grandfather
eventually leaving to attend boarding school

difficult_points: 句首分词短语修饰 Young；原文 Bom 很可能是 Born。
leaving ... 补充后续动作，to attend 表示离家的目的。

sentences.sentence: He had devoured books from the age of two, and through his own initiative he excelled at Latin, Greek, mathematics and natural philosophy.
sentences.translation: 他从两岁起便如饥似渴地读书，并凭自身主动性精通拉丁语、希腊语、数学和自然哲学。

structure.text: He had devoured books from the age of two
and through his own initiative
he excelled at Latin, Greek, mathematics and natural philosophy

difficult_points: devour books 是比喻，表示大量快速阅读。
excel at 表示擅长，后列举四个领域。

sentences.sentence: After leaving school, he was greatly encouraged by his mother's uncle, Richard Brock-lesby, a physician and Fellow of the Royal Society.
sentences.translation: 离校后，他受到母亲的叔父理查德·布罗克尔斯比的大力鼓励；后者是一名医生和皇家学会会士。

structure.text: After leaving school
he was greatly encouraged
by his mother's uncle, Richard Brock-lesby
a physician and Fellow of the Royal Society

difficult_points: After 后接动名词，逻辑主语为 he。
两个同位语逐层说明 uncle 身份；Brock-lesby 是源文断词。

sentences.sentence: Following Brocklesby's lead, Young decided to pursue a career in medicine.
sentences.translation: 杨追随布罗克尔斯比的道路，决定从医。

structure.text: Following Brocklesby's lead
Young decided to pursue a career in medicine

difficult_points: Following ... 的逻辑主语是 Young。
pursue a career in 表示从事某领域职业。

sentences.sentence: He studied in London, following the medical circuit, and then moved on to more formal education in Edinburgh, Gottingen and Cambridge.
sentences.translation: 他先在伦敦沿医学训练路径学习，随后前往爱丁堡、哥廷根和剑桥接受更正规教育。

structure.text: He studied in London
following the medical circuit
and then moved on to more formal education in Edinburgh, Gottingen and Cambridge

difficult_points: following ... 说明在伦敦的学习路径。
move on to 表示转向下一阶段。

sentences.sentence: After completing his medical training at the University of Cambridge in 1808, Young set up practice as a physician in London.
sentences.translation: 1808年在剑桥大学完成医学训练后，杨在伦敦开业行医。

structure.text: After completing his medical training at the University of Cambridge in 1808
Young set up practice as a physician in London

difficult_points: After + 动名词表示先完成训练再开业。
set up practice 是医生或律师开业的固定表达。

expressions.text: set up practice as a physician

sentences.sentence: He soon became a Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians and a few years later was appointed physician at St. George's Hospital.
sentences.translation: 他很快成为皇家内科医师学院会士，几年后又获任圣乔治医院医生。

structure.text: He soon became a Fellow of the Royal College of Physicians
and a few years later was appointed physician at St. George's Hospital

difficult_points: became 与 was appointed 是共用主语 He 的并列谓语。
后半句省略 He；be appointed physician 表示获任医生职务。

sentences.sentence: Young's skill as a physician, however, did not equal his skill as a scholar of natural philosophy or linguistics.
sentences.translation: 然而，杨作为医生的能力不及他作为自然哲学或语言学学者的能力。

structure.text: Young's skill as a physician
however
did not equal his skill as a scholar of natural philosophy or linguistics

difficult_points: equal 在此作动词，表示达到相同水平。
两个 as 短语分别说明其医生和学者身份。

sentences.sentence: Earlier, in 1801, he had been appointed to a professorship of natural philosophy at the Royal Institution, where he delivered as many as 60 lectures in a year.
sentences.translation: 更早的1801年，他已获任皇家研究院自然哲学教授，并在那里一年讲授多达60场讲座。

structure.text: he had been appointed to a professorship of natural philosophy at the Royal Institution
where he delivered as many as 60 lectures in a year

difficult_points: had been appointed 是过去完成时被动。
where 修饰 Royal Institution；as many as 强调数量多。

sentences.sentence: These were published in two volumes in 1807.
sentences.translation: 这些讲座于1807年分两卷出版。

structure.text: These
were published in two volumes

difficult_points: These 回指上一句的 lectures。
in two volumes 表示以两卷本形式。

sentences.sentence: In 1804 Young had become secretary to the Royal Society, a post he would hold until his death.
sentences.translation: 1804年，杨成为皇家学会秘书，并一直任职至去世。

structure.text: Young had become secretary to the Royal Society
a post
he would hold until his death

difficult_points: a post 与 secretary 职位同位。
he would hold 是省略 that 的定语从句修饰 post。

sentences.sentence: His opinions were sought on civic and national matters, such as the introduction of gas lighting to London and methods of ship construction.
sentences.translation: 在城市和国家事务上，人们会征询他的意见，例如伦敦引入煤气照明和船舶建造方法。

structure.text: His opinions
were sought on civic and national matters
such as the introduction of gas lighting to London and methods of ship construction

difficult_points: were sought 表示他的意见受到征询。
such as 后列举两类公共事务。

sentences.sentence: From 1819 he was superintendent of the Nautical Almanac and secretary to the Board of Longitude.
sentences.translation: 从1819年起，他担任《航海年历》主管和经度委员会秘书。

structure.text: From 1819
he
was superintendent of the Nautical Almanac and secretary to the Board of Longitude

difficult_points: 两个职务由 and 并列表语。
From 1819 表示任职起点。

sentences.sentence: From 1824 to 1829 he was physician to and inspector of calculations for the Palladian Insurance Company.
sentences.translation: 1824至1829年，他担任帕拉第安保险公司的医生兼计算审核员。

structure.text: From 1824 to 1829
he
was physician to and inspector of calculations for the Palladian Insurance Company

difficult_points: physician to 与 inspector of ... 是两个并列职务。
for the company 语义上关联两个职务。

sentences.sentence: Between 1816 and 1825 he contributed his many and various entries to the Encyclopedia Britan-nica, and throughout his career he authored numerous books, essays and papers.
sentences.translation: 1816至1825年间，他为《大英百科全书》撰写了大量不同词条；整个职业生涯中，他还创作了众多书籍、随笔和论文。

structure.text: Between 1816 and 1825
he contributed his many and various entries to the Encyclopedia Britan-nica
and throughout his career
he authored numerous books, essays and papers

difficult_points: and 连接两个各有时间状语的完整分句。
author 在此作动词“创作”；Britan-nica 是源文错误断词。

sentences.sentence: Young is a perfect subject for a biography — perfect, but daunting.
sentences.translation: 杨是传记的绝佳题材——虽完美，却也令人生畏。

structure.text: Young is a perfect subject for a biography
perfect, but daunting

difficult_points: 破折号后重复 perfect 并用 but 引出写作难度。
daunting 指其成就广博，使传记作者难以驾驭。

expressions.text: a perfect subject for a biography

sentences.sentence: Few men contributed so much to so many technical fields.
sentences.translation: 很少有人能对如此众多的技术领域作出如此大的贡献。

structure.text: Few men
contributed so much
to so many technical fields

difficult_points: Few 含否定意味，表示几乎没有人。
so much 与 so many 形成数量呼应。

sentences.sentence: Robinson's aim is to introduce non-scientists to Young's work and life.
sentences.translation: 罗宾逊的目标是向非科学专业读者介绍杨的工作与人生。

structure.text: Robinson's aim
is to introduce non-scientists to Young's work and life

difficult_points: 不定式短语作表语说明 aim 内容。
introduce somebody to something 表示使某人了解某事。

sentences.sentence: He succeeds, providing clear expositions of the technical material (especially that on optics and Egyptian hieroglyphs).
sentences.translation: 他成功做到了这一点，对技术材料作出了清晰阐释，尤其是光学和埃及象形文字部分。

structure.text: He succeeds
providing clear expositions of the technical material
especially that on optics and Egyptian hieroglyphs

difficult_points: providing ... 说明他如何成功。
that 代替 the technical material，避免重复。

sentences.sentence: Some readers of this book will, like Robinson, find Young's accom-plishments impressive; others will see him as some historians have — as a dilettante.
sentences.translation: 本书一些读者会像罗宾逊一样，认为杨的成就令人赞叹；另一些读者则会像某些历史学家那样，把他视为涉猎广而不精的业余者。

structure.text: Some readers of this book will
like Robinson
find Young's accom-plishments impressive
others will see him as some historians have
as a dilettante

difficult_points: find + 宾语 + 形容词构成复合宾语。
as some historians have 中 have 替代 seen him；原文 accom-plishments 断词异常。

sentences.sentence: Yet despite the rich material presented in this book, readers will not end up knowing Young personally.
sentences.translation: 然而，尽管本书材料丰富，读者最终仍无法真正了解杨的个人面貌。

structure.text: despite the rich material presented in this book
presented in this book
readers will not end up knowing Young personally

difficult_points: despite 后接名词短语表达让步。
end up doing 表示最终处于某种结果。

sentences.sentence: We catch glimpses of a playful Young, doodling Greek and Latin phrases in his notes on medical lectures and translating the verses that a young lady had written on the walls of a summerhouse into Greek elegiacs.
sentences.translation: 我们偶尔看到杨活泼的一面：他在医学讲座笔记里随手写下希腊语和拉丁语短句，还把一位年轻女士写在凉亭墙上的诗句译成希腊挽歌体。

structure.text: We catch glimpses of a playful Young
doodling Greek and Latin phrases in his notes on medical lectures
and translating the verses
that a young lady had written on the walls of a summerhouse

difficult_points: doodling 与 translating 并列修饰 Young。
that 从句修饰 verses，had written 表示写诗先于翻译。

sentences.sentence: Young was introduced into elite society, attended the theatre and learned to dance and play the flute.
sentences.translation: 杨被引入上流社会，常去剧院，并学会跳舞和吹笛子。

structure.text: Young was introduced into elite society
attended the theatre
and learned to dance and play the flute

difficult_points: 三个谓语并列，第一个被动，后两个主动。
dance 与 play 共用 learned to。

sentences.sentence: In addition, he was an accomplished horseman.
sentences.translation: 此外，他还是一名技艺高超的骑手。

structure.text: he
was an accomplished horseman

difficult_points: accomplished 在此表示技艺娴熟。
In addition 承接前句继续列举个人才能。

sentences.sentence: However, his personal life looks pale next to his vibrant career and studies.
sentences.translation: 然而，与他充满活力的事业和研究相比，他的个人生活显得苍白。

structure.text: his personal life
looks pale
next to his vibrant career and studies

difficult_points: look pale 是比喻，表示不够鲜明丰富。
next to 在此表示与……相比。

sentences.sentence: Young married Eliza Maxwell in 1804, and according to Robinson, "their marriage was a happy one and she appreciated his work," Almost all we know about her is that she sustained her husband through some rancorous disputes about optics and that she worried about money when his medical career was slow to take off.
sentences.translation: 杨于1804年与伊莱扎·麦克斯韦结婚；据罗宾逊说，“他们婚姻幸福，她也欣赏他的工作。”我们对她的了解几乎只有：她支持丈夫度过一些关于光学的激烈争论，并在他的医疗事业迟迟未起步时为钱担忧。

structure.text: Young married Eliza Maxwell in 1804
according to Robinson
their marriage was a happy one and she appreciated his work
Almost all we know about her
is
that she sustained her husband through some rancorous disputes about optics and that she worried about money when his medical career was slow to take off

difficult_points: 原文引语后的逗号错误连接新句，因此必须作为一个 Block 保留。
Almost all 后有省略 that 的定语从句 we know about her。
两个 that 从句并列作表语；take off 表示事业起步腾飞。

sentences.sentence: Very little evidence survives about the complexities of Young's relationships with his mother and father.
sentences.translation: 关于杨与父母关系的复杂情况，留存下来的证据极少。

structure.text: Very little evidence
survives
about the complexities of Young's relationships with his mother and father

difficult_points: Very little 含否定意味，表示证据几乎没有。
survives 指资料保存至今。

sentences.sentence: Robinson does not credit them, or anyone else, with shaping Young's extraordinary mind.
sentences.translation: 罗宾逊并不认为杨的父母或任何其他人塑造了他非凡的心智。

structure.text: Robinson
does not credit them, or anyone else, with shaping Young's extraordinary mind

difficult_points: credit somebody with doing 表示认为某人促成某事。
them 指 Young's mother and father；or anyone else 是插入补充。

sentences.sentence: Despite the lack of details concerning Young's rela-tionships, however, anyone interested in what it means to be a genius should read this book.
sentences.translation: 不过，尽管缺少关于杨人际关系的细节，任何对“成为天才意味着什么”感兴趣的人都应该阅读本书。

structure.text: Despite the lack of details concerning Young's rela-tionships
anyone
interested in what it means to be a genius
what it means to be a genius
should read this book

difficult_points: interested ... 修饰 anyone，what 从句作介词 in 的宾语。
原文 rela-tionships 被错误断词，须保留。

expressions.text: what it means to be a genius
